mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value less than 0.05, or rolling Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in greater than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in greater than 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side contraction that triggers short-term equity sell-offs, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive or flat during industrial production contractions, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in historical backtests.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising inflation and stable interest rates creates a negative real yield environment that erodes equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged
failure_condition: Negative real yield periods yield positive 20-day forward returns in greater than 58% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and supports corporate earnings trajectories, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with payroll growth with Rank IC below 0.01, or positive payroll surprises consistently precede negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction signals an economic recession that immediately depresses equity risk premiums, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in greater than 65% of quarters following negative GDP growth, or the signal fails to predict direction during confirmed NBER recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and payroll growth indicates productivity shocks that create short-term market uncertainty, leading to negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth exceeds PAYEMS growth by > 0.5 percentage points
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive during productivity divergence episodes, or the interaction term shows no predictive power with t-statistic below 1.5 in walk-forward validation.
